texto: Thinking has a quiet skin. But I feel the and of things inside it. Blue hills most gentle in calm light, then stretches of assailAnd ransack. Such tangles of charred wreckage, shrapnel-bits Singling and singeing where they fall. I feel the stumbling gait of what I am,The quiet uproar of undone, how to be hidden is a tempting, violent thing— Each thought breaking always in another.All the unlawful elsewheres rushing in.
explicacao: Citação de Laure Sheck sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Thinking has a quiet skin. But I feel the and of things inside it. Blue hills most gentle in calm…” articula reflexao, poetry, quiet em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
